_meta.title: Clinical Trial Counts by Therapeutic Condition Area
_meta.note: Queried April 2026. Counts include all study types (interventional + observational) across all statuses. Condition categories are mapped from ClinicalTrials.gov MeSH condition terms using keyword grouping. Numbers reflect total registered studies, not active-only.
_meta.how_to_refresh: Use ClinicalTrials.gov API v2: GET https://clinicaltrials.gov/api/v2/studies?query.cond={condition}&countTotal=true. Replace {condition} with condition terms like 'neoplasms' for oncology, 'cardiovascular diseases' for CV, etc. Aggregate results across related condition terms for each category.

oncology.notes: Largest therapeutic area. Includes all cancer types (solid tumors + hematologic).

cardiovascular.notes: Second largest area. Includes lipid disorders, arrhythmias, heart failure, CAD.

respiratory.notes: Includes inflammatory lung disease, COPD, lung cancer (counted in both oncology and respiratory).

metabolic.notes: GLP-1 agonist boom driving rapid growth.

cns_neurology.notes: High trial count but low approval rate due to BBB challenge.

hematology.notes: Some overlap with oncology for blood cancers.

dermatology.notes: Fast growth driven by IL-4/13 and IL-17 biologics.

immunology.notes: Overlap with dermatology (psoriatic arthritis) and hematology (autoimmune cytopenias).

rare_disease.notes: Count based on FDA Rare Disease designation cross-reference.

hepatology.notes: Rapid growth post-resmetirom approval for MASH.

ophthalmology.notes: Smallest category but high innovation density (intravitreal biologics, gene therapy).
